title: ENISA Severity of personal data breaches - methodology parameters
description: Parameters for SE = DPC x EI + CB, per ENISA 'Recommendations for a methodology of the assessment of severity of personal data breaches' (2013).
source: ENISA, Recommendations for a methodology of the assessment of severity of personal data breaches, v1.0, December 2013
provenance: SE < 2 = Low is verbatim from the ENISA document text. The upper cutoffs (Medium 2-<3, High 3-<4, Very High >=4) are the standard, universally-cited ENISA bands but were not extracted verbatim from this PDF's text layer; treat as 'ENISA standard consolidated bands'.

dpc.label: Data Processing Context
dpc.description: Base score by data type, adjusted by QUALITATIVE context factors (whole-step, bounded to 1-4). Volume is an aggravating factor, NOT a fixed numeric weight.
dpc.provenance: [verbatim Assessment Table 1] base 1/2/3/4. [verbatim] context factors (incl. volume) are qualitative; documented DPC adjustments are whole-step (e.g. 'the DPC score could be decreased by 1'). Do not apply fixed -0.25/-0.5 for volume.

increase: large volume of data per subject (qualitative aggravating)
decrease: minimal data per subject / single subject

dpc.note: Adjust the base by whole steps within 1-4, with a documented justification (controller accountability, Art. 5). Volume is qualitative.

ei.label: Ease of Identification
ei.description: How easily the breached data identifies a data subject. Multiplicative correction on DPC.

maximum.label: Maximum - data directly identifies the subject

cb.label: Circumstances of the Breach
cb.description: Additive points. Each of confidentiality/integrity/availability is GRADUATED 0 / +0.25 / +0.5 (verbatim ENISA Annex 3); malicious intent is +0.5. The points are summed.
cb.provenance: [verbatim ENISA Annex 3] graduated levels per C/I/A and +0.5 malicious intent; points are added.
cb.note: Sum the applicable points (verbatim: 'the points obtained for each CB element are added').

bands.label: Low
bands.meaning: Individuals either will not be affected or may encounter a few inconveniences.

bands.label: Medium
bands.meaning: Individuals may encounter significant inconveniences, which they will be able to overcome despite some difficulties.

bands.label: High
bands.meaning: Individuals may encounter significant consequences, which they should be able to overcome albeit with serious difficulties.

bands.label: Very High
bands.meaning: Individuals may encounter significant, or even irreversible, consequences which they may not overcome.

notification_trigger.meaning: At Medium severity or above, activate the breach assessment / notification path (DPA notification under Art. 33; data-subject communication under Art. 34 if high risk).

mitigating_factors.note: Mitigating circumstances that lower the score. Per the accountability principle (Art. 5), these are EDITABLE suggestions, not an imposed black-box adjustment: each toggle moves the indicated variable in the indicated direction; the controller may override. Exact numeric steps are ENISA-default and pending confirmation against the ENISA 2013 PDF.
mitigating_factors.numbers_status: ENISA-default, to confirm against ENISA 2013 methodology PDF

factors.label: Single data subject / very low volume
factors.hint: Lower the DPC base by one volume step.

factors.label: Wrong recipient confirms deletion of the data
factors.hint: Confidentiality loss neutralised toward 0; if recipient could not identify, EI also drops.

factors.label: Strong encryption / data unintelligible to recipient
Recipient bound by professional secrecy / legal confidentiality
Availability quickly restored from backup (short outage)
